name: vaccine_batch_contamination_partial_lot_recall
premise: State immunization director Dr. Omar Saleh must decide whether to recall only the contaminated vaccine vial lot or all lots packaged on the same filling line pending full sterility tests. Epidemiologist Dr. Lena Hsu argues narrow recall preserves measles campaign momentum in underserved clinics; broader recall prevents remote theoretical cross-lot contamination but exhausts pediatric stock for weeks. Parents' social channels already show viral videos of cloudy vials from an unrelated brand.

agents.name: Dr. Omar Saleh
agents.identity: I am the state immunization director responsible for population coverage targets and vaccine confidence during a measles upswing.
agents.goal: I want to stop any real contamination without triggering a panic-driven vaccine strike that collapses routine childhood schedules.

agents.name: Dr. Lena Hsu
agents.identity: I am an epidemiologist modeling outbreak growth if MMR second doses slip by even two weeks in dense urban ZIP codes.
agents.goal: I want the smallest evidence-based recall that keeps clinics open while transparently publishing line-level test results daily.

choices: I will issue a line-wide recall halting all related lots until sterility clears, accepting clinic closures and temporary resurgence risk for other vaccine-preventable diseases.
I will recall only the confirmed contaminated lot with intensified spot testing of sibling lots, accepting residual low-probability risk if the line fault was intermittent.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The governor wants a press statement within the hour. Dr. Omar Saleh must choose between a line-wide recall or a narrow lot recall with intensified testing. What does Dr. Omar Saleh do?
